name: new-ip
description: New leaf IP SSOT authoring: requirements ledger -> scaffold -> canonical SSOT YAML -> validation -> downstream handoff

tasks.content: [SSOT] Phase 1: Build requirements ledger and leaf boundary
tasks.detail: FIRST read the authoritative reference materials: (1) doc/wiki/atlas-new-ip-recipe.md — new-IP recipe with proven patterns; (2) doc/wiki/atlas-ssot-flag-reference.md — opt-in SSOT flags with effects and decision tree. Proven demo IPs (apb_compare/, apb_gpio_demo/, apb_pulse_counter/, atcuart_mini/, apb_xor_demo/) are OPTIONAL reference examples — browse one only if it resembles the new IP's shape (CSR + combinational, output mirror, input-driven counter, FSM + APB master, two-register XOR), and treat it as a worked example, not a parent the new IP inherits from. Do not couple the new IP to any demo IP. THEN read the user request and any local requirement/spec files. Extract: IP name, type, purpose, clock/reset scheme, bus/protocol interfaces, sideband/debug signals, parameters, memories/FIFOs, registers/CSRs, interrupts, function behavior, cycle/latency behavior, security/safety behavior, reset defaults, error behavior, ordering/backpressure rules, timing/performance constraints, power intent, debug/observability, integration ownership, DFT/testability assumptions, synthesis/PPA targets, verification intent, and production signoff expectations. Create a ledger in the SSOT content itself: decided facts, explicit assumptions, blocking gaps, and deferred enhancements. Ask only for blocking gaps; otherwise make conservative assumptions and record them in custom.assumptions. Decide which blocks are manifest sub_modules and which, if any, require child_ssot ownership.
tasks.criteria:
doc/wiki/atlas-new-ip-recipe.md was read
doc/wiki/atlas-ssot-flag-reference.md was read
The new IP's SSOT stands on its own — no implicit dependency on any demo IP's identity, registers, or scenarios
IP name and top_module.type are known
Clock/reset polarity and sync/async behavior are known or recorded as assumptions
Every external interface has protocol, role, width, direction, and backpressure/handshake rules
Function model and cycle model gaps are identified before writing RTL
Timing, power, security, error, integration, DFT, synthesis, DV, and quality-gate assumptions are explicit
Every behavioral ambiguity is either resolved, recorded as an assumption, or listed as a blocking question
No RTL/TB/sim files are created in ssot-gen

tasks.content: [SSOT] Phase 2: Scaffold only the leaf SSOT directory
tasks.detail: Create <ip>/yaml/ and standard empty output directories only if needed for workflow compatibility. Do not generate production RTL, testbench, simulation output, filelists, or documentation. If any existing scaffold files are present, treat them as placeholders and do not cite them as completed downstream work.
tasks.criteria:
<ip>/yaml/ exists
No production RTL/TB/sim artifact was generated by ssot-gen
Existing user files were not overwritten unless they are the canonical SSOT target
The leaf IP scope is separate from SoC/top integration scope

tasks.detail:
Write exactly one canonical SSOT at <ip>/yaml/<ip>.ssot.yaml using $ATLAS_WORKFLOW_ROOT/ssot-gen/rules/ssot-template.yaml. Fill all canonical sections with enough structured detail for downstream workflows to implement generically. Required depth: top_module identity; sub_modules with ownership and file intent; parameters with defaults/types/drives; io_list with every port; features with trigger/datapath/control/output; dataflow sequences; function_model with state variables, transactions, preconditions, outputs, side effects, error cases, invariants, and reference-model hints; cycle_model with clock/reset timing, latency bounds, handshake rules, pipeline stages, ordering, backpressure, and observability; clock_reset_domains; CDC/RDC; registers or explicit no-CSR note; memory instances or explicit none; interrupts; FSM states/transitions; timing/performance; power intent; security/safety; error_handling; debug_observability; integration contract; DFT/DFD assumptions; synthesis/PPA constraints; coding_rules; custom.assumptions; dir_structure; filelist intent; test_requirements with concrete scenarios, stimulus, expected results, checkers, coverage goals; quality_gates with pass criteria/evidence; traceability; workflow_todos with stage-specific TODOs in content/detail/command/script/instructions/criteria/source_refs format; generation_flow handoffs. Do not include IP-specific fixed implementation code.

CRITICAL GOTCHAS (each one was a real regression in this codebase — every new IP must observe them; see system_prompt.md and doc/wiki/atlas-ssot-flag-reference.md for full rationale):
  1. State variable names MUST NOT collide with register names. Use opa_q (not OPA), gpio_data (not DATA), count_q (not COUNT). Bind via state_variables[*].source = registers.<REG>.<field>.
  2. cycle_model.cosim: true is REQUIRED for any IP with multi-cycle state (counters, FSMs, accumulators, arbiters). Without it, multi-cycle state evolution is invisible to the scoreboard and silently passes.
  3. Every state_variables[*] backed by a register MUST set source: registers.<REG>.<field> so csr_write mirrors into FL state.
  4. transactions[*].preconditions: list specific transactions first, terminal FM_IDLE with preconditions: [] last. The first matching transaction wins.
  5. Every test_requirements.scenarios[*] MUST have a concrete stimulus_machine_spec (assign | csr_writes | timeline). Prose-only scenarios silently exercise wrong DUT inputs.
  6. state_variables[*].reset must be a numeric literal (15, (1 << WIDTH) - 1), never strings like "all-ones".
tasks.criteria:
<ip>/yaml/<ip>.ssot.yaml exists and is non-empty
YAML has all production required top-level sections
No live <TBD> remains unless explicitly approved by user
Every interface port has name, width, direction, description
Every feature has trigger, datapath/control behavior, and observable output
function_model defines state variables, transactions, preconditions, outputs, side effects, error cases, invariants, and scoreboard/reference-model guidance
Every non-reset function_model transaction has executable output_rules or state_updates, not prose-only outputs/side_effects
cycle_model defines clock/reset timing, latency bounds, handshake rules, pipeline stages, ordering/backpressure, and observability
timing, power, security, error_handling, debug_observability, integration, dft, and synthesis sections are substantive, not placeholders
workflow_todos.rtl-gen exists and every executable item has content, detail, command, script, instructions, criteria, source_refs, and owner_module/owner_file when inferable
Every scenario has stimulus, expected result, checker, and coverage intent
quality_gates defines pass criteria and evidence for SSOT, RTL, DV, coverage, and signoff

tasks.detail: Run python3 "$ATLAS_WORKFLOW_ROOT/ssot-gen/scripts/verify_ssot.py" <ip> --root "$ATLAS_PROJECT_ROOT" --mode engineering. Inspect failures literally. Fix missing sections, malformed YAML, weak scenarios, prose-only function_model transactions, missing port fields, missing Preview anchors, missing assumptions, or live TBD markers. Repeat until validation passes. Do not move to handoff based on prose confidence.
tasks.criteria:
Validation command executed and output captured
YAML parses successfully
Structural gate passes: substantive file, canonical sections, Preview-readable anchors, no unapproved live TBD markers
Any validator warning is fixed or explicitly documented as an accepted assumption
No downstream success is claimed in ssot-gen

tasks.detail: Output a compact [SSOT HANDOFF] for rtl-gen. Include: module, SSOT path, top type, clock/reset, interfaces, parameters, memories, registers/CSR policy, interrupts, function_model summary, cycle_model summary, timing/power/security/error/integration/DFT/synthesis assumptions, sub_modules ownership, verification scenarios, quality_gates, assumptions, constraints, and downstream definition of done. State that rtl-gen owns RTL/filelist/compile, tb-gen owns TB/sim/coverage, sim_debug owns VCD/coverage inspection, and EDA workflows own synthesis/STA/DFT evidence.
tasks.criteria:
[SSOT HANDOFF] block is emitted
Handoff includes exact SSOT path and module name
Handoff lists constraints and assumptions that affect RTL/TB/EDA behavior
Handoff includes quality_gates pass criteria and evidence requirements
Handoff does not claim RTL, TB, sim, lint, waveform, coverage, synthesis, STA, or DFT completion
Next workflow is explicitly rtl-gen
